'''Jamie Ainscough''' (born 20 July 1972) is an Australian former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1990s and 2000s. An [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]] international and [[New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales]] [[State of Origin series|State of Origin]] representative three-quarter back, he played his club football for [[Western Suburbs Magpies|Western Suburbs]], the [[Newcastle Knights]], the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]] and the [[Wigan Warriors]] (with whom he won the 2002 [[Challenge Cup]]).<ref>{{Cite book |last=Whiticker |first=Alan |title=The encyclopedia of rugby league players : every premiership player |date=1995 |publisher=G. Allen |isbn=1-875169-57-1 |location=Smithfield, N.S.W. |oclc=35304987 |author-link=Alan Whiticker}}</ref>

==Background==
Born in Sydney, Ainscough played for the Macquarie Cobras J.R.L.F.C in [[Cartwright, New South Wales]]. While attending [[Elderslie, New South Wales|Elderslie]] High School, Ainscough played for the [[Australia national schoolboys rugby league team|Australian Schoolboys team]] in 1989.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2007-10-10 |title=Australian Schoolboys |url=http://www.sportingpulse.com/assoc_page.cgi?c=7-2130-0-0-0&sID=26424 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080821104534/http://www.sportingpulse.com/assoc_page.cgi?c=7-2130-0-0-0&sID=26424 |archive-date=2008-08-21 |access-date=2008-10-10 |website=LeagueNet |publisher=SportingPulse}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===1990s===
The following year he was playing first grade in the [[New South Wales Rugby League premiership]] for [[Western Suburbs Magpies|Western Suburbs]]. Ainscough also played 87 games with the [[Newcastle Knights]] from 1992 to 1996. He made one international appearance for [[Australian national rugby league team|Australia]] against [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] in the 3rd Test of 1995 Brisbane. In that game Ainscough came on as a replacement and scored a try. Ainscough represented the [[New South Wales Rugby League team]] in the [[State of Origin series]] in 12 games between 1996 and 2001 (three as a replacement). Ainscough joined [[St. George Dragons|St George]] in 1997 and played on the wing in the [[1999 NRL Grand Final]] loss against the [[Melbourne Storm]].  St. George led 14-nil at half time but were run down by Melbourne. The illegal tackle he made on [[Craig Smith (rugby league born 1973)|Craig Smith]] resulted in a penalty try contributing to St. George losing the premiership.<ref>{{Cite web |last=McMurray |first=Adrian |date=2011-09-15 |title=Six most pivotal storm finals moments |url=http://www.nrl.com/six-most-pivotal-storm-finals-moments/tabid/10874/newsid/64826/default.aspx/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160915044758/http://www.nrl.com/six-most-pivotal-storm-finals-moments/tabid/10874/newsid/64826/default.aspx/ |archive-date=2016-09-15 |access-date=2016-09-04 |publisher=National Rugby League}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=First Grade Players |url=http://www.westsmagpies.com.au/the-archives/first-grade-players/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190908145822/http://www.westsmagpies.com.au/the-archives/first-grade-players/ |archive-date=2019-09-08 |access-date=2019-10-31 |website=Western Suburbs Magpies}}</ref>

===2000s===
On 24 October 2000, Ainscough was awarded the [[Australian Sports Medal]] for his achievements.<ref>{{Cite It's an Honour |ausawardid=971428 |date=2000-10-24 |recipient=Mr Jamie AINSCOUGH |award=Australian Sports Medal |citation=Current Australian Kangaroo player |accessdate=2021-04-02 }}</ref> He played 106 games with [[St George Dragons|St George]] or the merged [[St George Illawarra Dragons]] up until 2001 at centre or wing. Ainscough concluded his career in the [[Super League]] with English side the [[Wigan Warriors]] in 2002 when they won the [[Challenge Cup]]. He played in a Wigan Warriors team studded with Australians in [[Brett Dallas]], [[Julian O'Neill]], [[Adrian Lam]], [[Craig Smith (rugby league born 1971)|Craig Smith]], [[David Furner]] and coached by [[Stuart Raper]]. Following that season he went close to needing to have his arm amputated following an infection that was found to have been caused after one of [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] centre [[Martin Gleeson (rugby league)|Martin Gleeson]]'s teeth was left embedded in Ainscough's arm.<ref>{{Cite news |last=Richards |first=Martin |date=2004-04-28 |title=A tooth stuck in head – but hooker plays on |work=The Guardian |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2004/apr/28/rugbyleague.comment |url-status=live |access-date=2013-09-08 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140102195902/https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2004/apr/28/rugbyleague.comment |archive-date=2014-01-02}}</ref><ref>{{Cite news |last=Gleeson |first=Martin |author-link=Martin Gleeson (rugby league) |date=2009-08-22 |title=26 August 2002: Wigan player has tooth removed – from arm |work=The Guardian |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2009/sep/22/wigan-tooth-removed-from-arm |url-status=live |access-date=2015-10-02 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20151001013534/https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2009/sep/22/wigan-tooth-removed-from-arm |archive-date=2015-10-01}}</ref>
